title: Primary job selection
principles: The primary job must describe the complete practical attack pattern rather than the single strongest isolated statistic.
Sustained damage requires repeated useful pressure across firing and reload cycles; a sustained rhythm alone does not automatically make it the primary job.
Focused damage requires evidence that the attack pattern concentrates value on one target without stronger multi-target or precision evidence.
Group clear requires reliable multi-target reach through radial, chaining, spread, Punch Through, or another supplied mechanism.
Area control requires evidence that the weapon influences space, movement, persistent zones, grouping, or repeated area denial; radial damage alone is insufficient.
Status application should be primary only when both status relationship and repeated valid application opportunities are central to the attack pattern.
Precision attacks require explicit accuracy, scoped, weak-point, headshot, or equivalent evidence and must not be inferred from semi-automatic behavior alone.
Heavy attacks apply only to melee or equivalent supplied heavy-attack behavior and require explicit heavy-attack evidence.
General use is appropriate when no more specific job is clearly supported or when several profiles are balanced without a dominant pattern.
For multi-mode weapons, choose the job of the dominant practical mode and mention materially different secondary roles separately.

sustained_damage: Repeated pressure across complete firing cycles is the best-supported function.
focused_damage: The supplied attack pattern primarily concentrates damage on individual targets.
group_clear: The supplied delivery reliably reaches multiple enemies as a central function.
area_control: The supplied mechanic influences or denies space beyond simply damaging several enemies.
status_application: Repeated valid status application is the dominant supported function.
precision_attacks: Explicit precision or weak-point evidence defines the practical role.
heavy_attacks: Explicit heavy-attack evidence defines the melee role.
general_use: No narrower role is sufficiently dominant from the supplied evidence.

exceptions: Do not select group_clear from an aoe tag alone.
Do not select area_control from radial damage alone.
Do not select status_application from status chance alone.
Do not select precision_attacks from accuracy or semi-automatic behavior alone without precision evidence.
Do not select heavy_attacks merely because generic heavy-attack fields exist for every melee weapon.
